modules.name: kg_detective.constants
documentation:
Constants used in package

Attributes:

: PKG_INDICES (dict): Key is language abbreviation, such as `en`, `es`. Value is the name of pre-trained package

functions.name: kg_detective.lib.merge_range
documentation:
Merge two tuples, which have two elements respectively 

Args:
  first (tuple): (start, end)
  second (tuple): (start, end)

Returns:
  list: merged tuples


Examples:
  >>> merge_range((1, 3), (4, 8))
  [(1, 3), (4, 8)]

  >>> merge_range((1, 3), (3, 8))
  [(1, 3), (3, 8)]

  >>> merge_range((1, 3), (2, 8))
  [(1, 8)]

functions.name: kg_detective.lib.merge
documentation:
Merge a list of tuples, which have two elements respectively 

Args:
  ranges (list): [(start1, end1), (start2, end2), ...]

Returns:
  list: merged tuples


Examples:
  >>> merge([(1, 4),(3, 6), (4, 7), (9, 12)])
  [(1, 7), (9, 12)]
